definition: Single-cell transcriptomic atlas of ageing mouse tissues across 23 organs at six age groups (1, 3, 18, 21, 24, and 30 months); also called Mouse Ageing Cell Atlas
Cyclin-dependent kinase inhibitor 2A gene; encodes the tumour suppressor protein p16 and is one of the most widely used markers of cellular senescence
Protein product of the Cdkn2a gene; a cyclin-dependent kinase inhibitor used as a senescence marker
Sirtuin gene family (Sirt3, Sirt4, Sirt5); NAD-dependent deacetylases implicated in delaying cellular senescence
Cells in the kidney glomerulus that provide structural support and help regulate glomerular blood flow and filtration
Gene encoding the alpha-1 subunit of the sodium/potassium ATPase; important for kidney glomerular filtration
Epidermal growth factor gene; its expression in the kidney is relevant to glomerular filtration rate
Gene encoding uromodulin (Tamm-Horsfall protein); the most abundant protein in normal urine, produced by kidney loop of Henle epithelial cells
Joining chain gene (also known as Igj); a component of polymeric immunoglobulins and a marker of plasma cells
Activator protein 1; a transcription factor complex composed of JUN and FOS family proteins, involved in cell proliferation and differentiation
Interleukin-1 beta gene; encodes a key pro-inflammatory cytokine involved in inflammation and cellular senescence
C-type lectin domain family 4 member F; a specific marker gene for liver-resident Kupffer cells
Liver-resident macrophages that line the hepatic sinusoids and play roles in immune surveillance and tissue homeostasis
Mannose receptor C-type 1 gene; encodes a receptor involved in immune defence, expressed in Kupffer cells and liver sinusoidal endothelial cells
Liver sinusoidal endothelial cells; specialized endothelial cells lining the hepatic sinusoids with roles in endocytosis and immune function
External RNA Controls Consortium; synthetic RNA spike-in controls used to estimate technical noise and error rates in RNA-seq experiments
Major histocompatibility complex class I; cell surface molecules that present intracellular peptides for immune surveillance by cytotoxic T cells
Ferritin heavy chain 1 gene; involved in intracellular iron storage and associated with neurodegeneration in ageing microglia
A group of B lymphocytes sharing highly similar B cell receptor sequences, indicating descent from a common progenitor cell through clonal expansion
A group of T cells sharing identical or highly similar T cell receptor sequences, indicating common clonal origin
